source_pr.title: stats: use constant rand seed to make cm sktech test stable

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it used randomized input (Zipf distribution seeded by the current time), so the generated CMSketch/error distribution varied across runs and the probabilistic assertion could intermittently fail. This PR stabilizes the test by switching to constant seeds for the random generator (and slightly adjusting the assertion threshold) so the test input and expected error bounds are deterministic.
fix_pattern: Use deterministic test input: replace time-based randomness with fixed seeds (or log/accept a seed) so probabilistic tests become reproducible and stable.
